pr.title: Integration PR #143 — Self-Model Population v1
pr.description:
## Summary

First integration milestone (M1): wire Axiom's existing structural producer (`code-inventory`) into its existing semantic consumers (`capability_knowledge_graph`, `capability_relationship`) so Axiom can answer real repository dependency questions **from its own populated graph** instead of from an ad-hoc script or manual source parsing.

**This is not a new framework.** No new object family, registry, engine, or evidence system — only a deterministic import-edge extractor on the producer plus a thin adapter that hands plain `dict` payloads to the existing engines' `create()` APIs.

### What became executable
Before: the capability graph held only the 4 sample/Revit-primitive nodes and could not answer "what depends on module X". After: it holds the real **157 modules / 274 import edges / 9 isolated modules**, queryable via the CLI.

```
axiom self-model-build            # scan repo -> populate existing graph + relationship engines
axiom self-model-query --graph <id>                 # what modules exist / what imports what / which are isolated
axiom self-model-query --graph <id> --module <name> # what <name> depends on  +  what depends on <name>
```

### How it maps onto existing models (no new types)
- module → `CAPABILITY` graph node (`node_id == source_id == module name`, so edges resolve by name)
- import edge → `BUILDS_ON` graph edge
- import edge → `DEPENDS_ON` capability relationship

```python
# producer (codebase_inventory.py): deterministic, AST-based, src-internal only
def extract_import_edges(self) -> list[tuple[str, str]]: ...   # sorted, deduped, no self-edges

# adapter (self_model.py): thin coordinator, no persistent state
SelfModelBuilder(repo).populate_graph()          # -> CapabilityKnowledgeGraphEngine.create(nodes, edges)
SelfModelBuilder(repo).populate_relationships()  # -> CapabilityRelationshipEngine.create(relationships, known_ids)
graph_dependents(report, module)                 # answers read off the persisted report
```

Orphan/island detection is preserved by reusing the engines' built-in logic (`orphan_node_ids` / `orphan_capability_ids`); the adapter adds none of its own.

## What changed
- `src/axiom_core/codebase_inventory.py`: add `extract_import_edges()` + `_resolve_import_targets()` (read-only, deterministic).
- `src/axiom_core/self_model.py` (new): `SelfModelBuilder` + query helpers (`graph_modules/imports/isolated/dependencies/dependents`).
- `src/axiom_cli/main.py`: add `self-model-build`, `self-model-query`.
- `src/axiom_core/runner/command_registry.py`: catalog the two new READ_ONLY/SAFE commands.
- `tests/test_self_model.py` (new): fixed-subgraph fixture (4 modules / 2 edges / 1 island) proving node/edge/orphan/relationship counts, mapping, determinism, query answers, and before/after.

## What behavior changed
A new autonomous behavior: Axiom answers "what depends on a module?" from its own graph. No existing behavior changed; the producer and consumer engines are unmodified except for the additive extractor.

## What did NOT change
No new framework/object-model/registry/evidence system; no broad refactor; no shared-base extraction; no M2/M3/M4 work; the 2024 Revit baseline is untouched.

## Validation
- Targeted: `pytest tests/test_self_model.py` → **12 passed**; `pytest -k "inventory or codebase"` → 219 passed.
- Full suite: `poetry run pytest -q` → **4929 passed, 1 skipped** (preexisting skip).
- Lint: `poetry run ruff check src/ tests/` → **exit 0**.
- CLI smoke (real repo) answers all four required questions:
  - what modules exist → **157**
  - what imports what → **274 edges**
  - which modules are isolated → **9** (e.g. `axiom_core.discovery.harness`, `axiom_core.inventory`)
  - what depends on `axiom_core.codebase_inventory` → `axiom_cli.main`, `axiom_core.patch_impact_analyzer`, `axiom_core.self_model`

Graph and relationship engines agree: 274 edges / 9 orphans on both sides.

## Known risks / pending
- Edge semantics are module-level import edges (not symbol-level call graphs) — sufficient for M1's dependency questions.
- No Revit live validation required (introspection-only; 2024 baseline unaffected).

## Strategic fit
Closes broken transition BT-1 (structural self-model → semantic self-model). Strengthens evidence/knowledge integration by routing a structural discovery artifact into the capability registries rather than leaving it an isolated run output.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Integration PR #143 — Self-Model Population v1

Add an adapter that pipes code-inventory's real import graph into the existing
capability_knowledge_graph and capability_relationship engines so Axiom can
answer dependency questions from its own populated graph.

- code-inventory: add deterministic extract_import_edges() (AST, src-internal)
- self_model.py: thin adapter mapping modules->CAPABILITY nodes,
  imports->BUILDS_ON edges / DEPENDS_ON relationships; query helpers
- CLI: self-model-build, self-model-query (no new framework/registry)
- tests: fixed-subgraph fixture proving node/edge/orphan/relationship counts

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
